galactic.description: Legacy scalar sky background in visual magnitude per arcsec^2. When skyglow is omitted, this is the total moonless clear-sky background. When skyglow is present, this is treated as the natural moonless baseline used to infer the artificial skyglow residual.

skyglow.description: Optional moonless clear-sky total site brightness in visual magnitude per arcsec^2, including artificial light pollution. Lower values are brighter. May be a scalar or an FPA-shaped two-dimensional magnitude field. SatSim converts galactic and skyglow to linear photoelectrons and uses their difference as the artificial skyglow component; skyglow must not be numerically larger than galactic.

moon.description: Optional lunar sky-brightness mode. none disables lunar sky brightness. default currently aliases krisciunas-schaefer, a V-band scattered moonlight model using lunar phase, Moon-sky separation, Moon zenith distance, target zenith distance, and atmospheric extinction.

twilight.description: Optional below-horizon solar twilight sky-brightness mode. none disables twilight. default currently aliases patat, a V-band zenith twilight model using Sun zenith distance.

daytime.description: Optional above-horizon daylight sky-brightness mode. none disables daytime sky brightness. default currently aliases hosek-wilkie, a clear-sky daylight model with solar-elevation-dependent absolute normalization and angular sky shape. perez remains available as an explicit compact CIE/Perez comparison mode.

mode.description: Lunar sky-brightness mode. none disables lunar sky brightness. default aliases krisciunas-schaefer.
Twilight sky-brightness mode. none disables twilight sky brightness. default aliases patat.

Daytime sky-brightness mode. none disables daytime sky brightness. default aliases hosek-wilkie. hosek-wilkie uses the Hošek-Wilkie clear-sky model with internal turbidity and ground-albedo defaults. perez uses the older fixed-normalization CIE/Perez comparison path.
